title: Comparing economic growth across countries
subtitle: Development in per capita GDP from 2015 to 2024

scenes.text: Each dot here shows one of the countries classified as having low income in 1990 showing their level of income in 2015. By then, Guyana had just transitioned into an upper-middle income country. Vietnam and Nigeria had made it to lower-middle income, while Ethiopia was still a low-income country.
In the decade that followed, most countries experienced economic growth. But some had experienced a decline in GDP per capita, having a lower GDP per capita in 2024 compared to 2015.
And when we zoom out to compare with the rest of the world, it becomes very clear that there is still substantial catching up to be done.

It is therefore also relevant to look at how countries have performed relative to similar countries. The progress measure does exactly that, seeing whether the countries have performed better or worse than their peers.
We can also look at how the economies have grown across regions.
